**10. The Great Wall of China**

The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications built by the Chinese Empire from the 1st century BC to the 1st century AD. It stretches over 13,180 miles (21,000 km) and is one of the most impressive architectural achievements of the ancient world. The wall is a testament to the ingenuity and perseverance of the Chinese people, who built it to protect their country from invaders and to demonstrate their power and civilization.

**11. The Great Pyramid of Giza**

The Great Pyramid of Giza is a ancient Egyptian monument that is one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World. It is a massive limestone pyramid built around 2580 BC and is one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World. The pyramid is a testament to the ingenuity and engineering skills of the ancient Egyptians, who built it to demonstrate their mastery of mathematics, astronomy, and architecture.

**12. The Taj Mahal**

The Taj Mahal is a white marble mausoleum built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife, Mumtaz Mahal. The mausoleum is a masterpiece of Mughal architecture and is one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World. The Taj Mahal is a testament to the love and devotion of the Mughal Empire and is a symbol of the beauty and grandeur of Indian culture.
